pains.summary: AEs and SDRs don't attribute things correctly, creating data gaps
pains.quote: my AEs and SDRs don't attribute things correctly. So there's gaps I'm missing
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: Account matching breaks campaign attribution due to duplicates and data quality issues
pains.quote: The hard path is when it doesn't match. So that breaks this whole, you know, campaign attribution. And it's, it doesn't, it happens a lot more than most people will admit that you can't match this, and therefore it's a, it's a duplicate, it's a fat finger, whatever it is
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: New demand gen people over-inflate MQL numbers, worsening conversion metrics
pains.quote: they're, they're wanting to over count just because they're trying to hit the MQL number. And I, I'm, no, don't do that. You know, it's, the more you inflate the MQL number, the, the worse the conversion metrics is gonna be because you have people in there, it shouldn't be in there
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: Finance team owns RevOps but doesn't understand operations, creating organizational friction
pains.quote: we have our Financ team that owns Rev ops, but they don't understand operations. So that's a separate problem
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: Piecemealing attribution together with partial Tableau license
pains.quote: I'm piece mailing it together with a partial license of Tableau
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: Need to prove marketing engagement before partner deal reg to show shared value
pains.quote: We have to prove that we engage with the account or the individual so that there's some type of shared value and that both marketing touch
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: Previous tool Demand Ramp required heavy lift with codes in Salesforce and Marketo
pains.quote: the biggest problem was, is the, what was required from the system. So what's required from your system to be inside my systems at, at Salesforce and Marketo, HubSpot, all that, in order to connect all these dots. Is it, it was, it was a heavy lift
pains.speaker: Dan Edson

pains.summary: Channel classification was messy with offline sources and form submissions getting misclassified
pains.quote: So there are two forms or two campaigns here that operate like a form, but I'm one, I'm worried we're gonna have like more of these that come up. Right. And just to just use campaign name logic wouldn't be scalable for us.
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Email marketing attribution was inflated because deliveries and opens were counted as touchpoints
pains.quote: So obviously email marketing, we do a lot from across outreach and HubSpot. And I'm guessing, you know, that's probably the reason, just pure volume of emails that we send that's maybe getting so much attribution.
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Data quality concerns prevented adoption of predictive AI models
pains.quote: I think folks want a little bit more of like, let's keep it simple approach for now and not introduce anything that complex that, you know, executive show the executives they want to understand. more of the nuances behind machine learning model they want to know metric science effectiveness you know how real is it right
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Product was overwhelming for marketers to adopt without heavy guidance
pains.quote: there was almost so much that it was overwhelming for a lot of folks they had so many questions or they didn't really know where to go to access things and it took me time to you know learn it from harry and francisco to get to know where i was comfortable and you know if i'm the main person in here if i'm the only one in here then it becomes you know less valuable
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Custom dimension building was time-consuming with no clone feature
pains.quote: from like an administrative standpoint it was time consuming to build kind of dimensions and um you know it was there was no clone feature to easily you know configure those so if you have a lot of logic it was con time it was time consuming
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Event attribution timing was backwards due to lead creation before campaign member addition
pains.quote: the way they like to think about lead is, for example, these leads are often are created, they're gonna have to be created before they're added to the campaign because we like get them at the event, right? And then we'd load them into Salesforce and then they're tied to the campaign. So they're like always gonna be created before the campaign is tied to it, right?
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: RevSure's propensity scores didn't live in Salesforce or Outreach, making operationalization difficult
pains.quote: I think the issue is it doesn't live in a place and operationalizing, it was difficult. You know, it needs to be inside of Salesforce or outreach.
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: RevSure lacked intent data from sources like Calais, relying only on basic firmographics
pains.quote: It's not just about the basic thermographics that are on the lead itself. Like we need more information coming from other locations and Redshirt doesn't have that data.
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: RevSure's UI had too many bells and whistles that became a distraction for users
pains.quote: some of the other kind of bells and whistles in Revsure you know became sort of I'll say a distraction where it's like you know where should we focus our time on like getting value from a tool like this
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Dashboards sit unrefreshed; users need proactive insights delivered to them
pains.quote: getting away from dashboards that sit unrefreshed and into the world where agents are running analysis and just giving it into your inbox or in Slack or something so you don't have to go find that dashboard and refresh it. You're just getting answers fed to you, basically.
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Building dimensions in RevSure was time-consuming with no cloning feature
pains.quote: there wasn't you know a cloning feature for example like it's very time consuming to build a dimension you know for another aspect is like cohort intelligence we wanted to use heavily but you kind of want to adjust with complex and or filter logic from that module itself but every time you wanted complex and or logic you have to go build a dimension which was very time consuming and there was no way to clone existing dimensions to save time
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: RevSure's modular template approach restricted dashboard flexibility compared to report-based building
pains.quote: redshirt was very modular it was very much like here's the templates that you can use inside to create a dashboard
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Old school marketers resistant to modern attribution approaches, only understand first-touch or last-touch
pains.quote: some of the old school marketers just aren't used to this kind of new approach of like, let's classify every touch point and then, you know, give that you know put it inside of a platform that consolidates all the touch points across your map and crm and then do an attribution model i think this is just kind of new for a lot of folks so they are still like first touch or last touch
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

pains.summary: Organization not ready to adopt sophisticated attribution; needs simpler starting point
pains.quote: we can't even get people to get on board with the basics, then we can't get them on board with adopting this level of sophistication.
pains.speaker: Zach Matek

tools_switched_from.reason: Partial license, insufficient for joining objects needed for attribution
tools_switched_from.quote: I'm piece mailing it together with a partial license of Tableau. So I see the value in this
tools_switched_from.speaker: Dan Edson

tools_switched_from.reason: Used for account matching but still has hard path matching problems
tools_switched_from.quote: we have lean data where we're doing, you know, we lean heavily upon that, where it's, this account is known and it's in our account object, and leads come in and we have to do a matching
tools_switched_from.speaker: Dan Edson

tools_switched_from.reason: Switching to HockeyStack for simplicity, agent-driven insights, and easier configuration; RevSure too complex and modular for current organizational maturity
tools_switched_from.quote: We're going with a hockey stack as well.
tools_switched_from.speaker: Zach Matek

political_tussles.summary: Finance team owns RevOps but doesn't understand operations; marketing trying to gain control; finance dismisses marketing attribution
political_tussles.quote: there's actually a coup trying to happen here where we're trying to get control of the rev ops team under marketing ops because the finance guys can just, so they're building everything towards their skew, which is opportunities and you know, data modeling up there. But they don't really care or understand our top of the Funnel and how we're getting things there and the campaign attribution
political_tussles.speaker: Dan Edson

political_tussles.summary: Sales tools (SDR team names, Orum call platform) are 'in front of the line' ahead of marketing attribution tools due to IPO readiness
political_tussles.quote: Right now sales teams like we need SDR team names, we need Orum, we need a call platform. So that's like in front of the line
political_tussles.speaker: Dan Edson

contrarians.claim: RevSure doesn't need primary contact on opportunity for attribution - looks at all account contacts
contrarians.why_unusual: Goes against standard CRM attribution requiring contact roles on opportunities
contrarians.quote: We don't care if you have a primary contact, you know, that's associated with an opportunity, right? We're gonna look at all the marketing touches that occurred within that account, right?
contrarians.speaker: Jonathan Hill

contrarians.claim: Don't need Salesforce campaigns to track attribution - need clean UTM parameters instead
contrarians.why_unusual: Challenges conventional Marketo/Salesforce campaign-based attribution model
contrarians.quote: No, you don't. 'cause a lot of this is like LinkedIn ads campaigns, Google ads, you know, et cetera. Sometimes there is a Salesforce campaign to match. I mean, for us, what we really need is like clean UTM parameters around your digital campaigns
contrarians.speaker: Jonathan Hill

contrarians.claim: Email marketing should NOT get attribution for deliveries or opens, only clicks
contrarians.why_unusual: Most attribution platforms count email opens as meaningful engagement; client pushed back hard on this
contrarians.quote: I mean, is there a way to like filter, but that kind of gets complex with the dimensions. I mean maybe, so right now it's including NHS send like a deliver a delivered email, basically.
contrarians.speaker: Zach Matek

contrarians.claim: Direct traffic may be overstated due to ad blockers, and should be redistributed
contrarians.why_unusual: Client proactively questioned whether direct attribution was inflated and wanted to disperse it to actual channels
contrarians.quote: Remember that because of the la you know, limits to what you can do with ad tracking and people running ad blockers and all that sort of stuff. That direct may be overstated, right? But it certainly includes, you know, people who, who are searching for you
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: MQL scoring should be rebuilt based on demographic factors rather than source/op-source
contrarians.why_unusual: Client wanted to move away from traditional lead source-based scoring to firmographic/behavioral propensity
contrarians.quote: we're trying to do MQL rescoring, right, and think about what actually helps or contributes to a more positive, or what should our MQL score be so that we are prioritizing the right leads, right? So it's like looking more demographically than like what was the source of the op, right?
contrarians.speaker: Zach Matek

contrarians.claim: Intent signals are not forward predictive; freezing intent today and checking conversion 6 months later shows very low correlation
contrarians.why_unusual: Goes against the common RevTech vendor narrative that intent data is highly predictive of conversion; argues most intent is backward-looking or contemporaneous, not forward-looking
contrarians.quote: most intent signals don't, in my experience, and I've shown it to customers, are not forward predictive. Like you freeze the intent signal today and see what happens six months later, usually the correlation is very low.
contrarians.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

contrarians.claim: Intent is great for targeting, not for focus and prioritization; macro GTM motion factors override intent signals
contrarians.why_unusual: Contradicts the typical 'intent-based prioritization' playbook; argues firmographic and activity data within GTM motion dynamics matter more than intent for prioritization
contrarians.quote: intent is great for targeting, not necessarily for focus and prioritization. So for example, when it's in your GTM motion, the macro factors of your GTM motion take over more significantly. That's what this propensity model is really telling you.
contrarians.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

contrarians.claim: Propensity model with basic firmographics and activity data can outperform intent-enriched models because GTM motion dynamics dominate
contrarians.why_unusual: Pushes back against the 'more data sources = better model' orthodoxy; argues simpler model with right temporal framing beats complex intent-enriched models
contrarians.quote: even with the formographic, and this is not just a formographic, there's a lot of activity information and touch information, et cetera, within this, the dynamics of your GTM motion I actually take over a lot of the intent signals
contrarians.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

revsure_problems.problem: Client confused about difference between stage-by-stage velocity and stage duration metrics
revsure_problems.quote: So, you know, you see like 1 14 1 looking at current and previous month and if I go to duration and stage, you know, much higher. So I wanted to understand what the difference is there
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Form submissions incorrectly showing up in 'other campaigns' instead of being attributed to previous touchpoint
revsure_problems.quote: I would essentially would there'd be like the marketing form fill website, demorest, right. Just attribute to the touchpoint previous
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Lift test showing campaign names rather than channels in dropdown
revsure_problems.quote: the values here look like they're more campaign names rather than web, web paid webinar
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Need median calculation option instead of averages due to outlier sensitivity
revsure_problems.quote: We are very easily skewed by outliers. So I know these are averages. Is there any way to like create like a calculated version of that that's like a median
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Timeline journey shows 'lead' twice (lead creation and contact conversion) without clear labeling
revsure_problems.quote: when I'm in the timeline journey, I notice it will go like lead, push meeting and then hit lead again. And I, I think this is because this is the, I mean this corresponds with the, the date of the conversion from a lead to contact
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Service account read-only access to Salesforce still pending due to license availability
revsure_problems.quote: I think the only thing that was holding it back was making sure we had licenses available
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Funnel conversion attribution percentages appeared anomalously low and sorting was broken
revsure_problems.quote: I mean, ultimately, just these numbers seem very low than what they used to be. a month or two ago, so trying to understand what happened. For optimal, for example, and you can see across the board, these numbers are just very low. I tried playing around with different time frames.
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Account region filter not populating correctly in account prioritization
revsure_problems.quote: why the account region filter is not populating, but yeah, so
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Client struggled to explain FCA percentages to stakeholders without clear documentation
revsure_problems.quote: For this to be usable, you need to, you know, can't show a number of percent without having a good explanation for how it's being derived or what it means.
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Propensity scores not natively integrated into Salesforce/Outreach; write-back felt like extra cost
revsure_problems.quote: That's an extra cost for us, right? Like that's if this is the best thing to offer, that should be table stakes and that should be included, you know, base contract.
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Lack of intent data enrichment from external sources like Calais
revsure_problems.quote: we're able to get more like intent data from Calais and other places, more signals that I think are important that weren't included in the platform, right? It's not just about the basic thermographics that are on the lead itself. Like we need more information coming from other locations and Redshirt doesn't have that data.
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: UI too complex with bells and whistles that distract from core value; not simple enough for agents
revsure_problems.quote: some of the other kind of bells and whistles in Revsure you know became sort of I'll say a distraction where it's like you know where should we focus our time on like getting value from a tool like this
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: No cloning feature for dimensions; building dimensions very time-consuming
revsure_problems.quote: there wasn't you know a cloning feature for example like it's very time consuming to build a dimension
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Cohort intelligence module required building separate dimensions for complex AND/OR logic instead of inline filtering
revsure_problems.quote: cohort intelligence we wanted to use heavily but you kind of want to adjust with complex and or filter logic from that module itself but every time you wanted complex and or logic you have to go build a dimension which was very time consuming
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Modular template-based dashboard building too restrictive; can't pull report as baseline for dashboard
revsure_problems.quote: redshirt was very modular it was very much like here's the templates that you can use inside to create a dashboard
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Every change required vendor turnaround; customer couldn't self-serve configuration
revsure_problems.quote: every time we wanted to make a change, you know, you guys were very good at turnaround times, but it just felt like there was, you know, not a lot we could own as the customer to make those changes.
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

revsure_problems.problem: Automated report generation and scheduling not prominent/usable enough at time of initial use
revsure_problems.quote: probably was at the time we started using really just wasn't the same functionality
revsure_problems.speaker: Zach Matek

best_about_revsure.summary: Lightweight implementation compared to previous heavy-lift tools
best_about_revsure.quote: we can have data blowing and integrated from your map, your CRM, your sales engagement tool, your A BM tool and the various different third party ads counts that you have in about 20 minutes normally. So it's, I mean super, super, super lightweight
best_about_revsure.speaker: Jonathan Hill

best_about_revsure.summary: Pipeline projections and AI capabilities superior to competitors in bake-off
best_about_revsure.quote: Rev sure by far has I think the best, you know, AI capabilities and I'd really like to add as much machine learning and AI capabilities as we can to be more predictive
best_about_revsure.speaker: Zach Matek

best_about_revsure.summary: Client praised Francisco, Alex, and Harry specifically as wonderful to work with
best_about_revsure.quote: working with this team was wonderful you know have only good things to say about francisco alex and harry
best_about_revsure.speaker: Zach Matek

best_about_revsure.summary: Pipeline projection accuracy was strong with ~5% margin of error in normal quarters
best_about_revsure.quote: when we had a more conventional quarter i think there was like a five percent margin of error which is pretty good
best_about_revsure.speaker: Zach Matek

best_about_revsure.summary: Forward validation of propensity model showed 5x conversion difference between high and low buckets
best_about_revsure.quote: high propensity leads are converting like you know here you could just say 5x compared to the low propensity leads
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: Machine learning capabilities were the best part of RevSure platform
best_about_revsure.quote: i don't doubt the machine learning capabilities of the platform i think that was probably the best uh But of the tool and the reason to work with you guys
best_about_revsure.speaker: Zach Matek

best_about_revsure.summary: Deep respect for Zach as user and champion who provided valuable feedback
best_about_revsure.quote: we really respect you as a user, Zach, and you've kind of, you know, given us so much help, feedback. I know you've been a champion of the overall platform.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure's analytics stronger and more sophisticated than competitors
best_about_revsure.quote: all our other customers say that our analytics is stronger
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure's data platform configurability (entity resolution, web tracking, custom attribution) unmatched in market
best_about_revsure.quote: I don't think any other company, I don't know, maybe CalibreMine, I'm not sure, has this level of configurability on the underlying data platform and the underlying data
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

immediate.win: Funnel views already getting positive reception from stakeholders
immediate.quote: I think we've already got some relative positive reception from, you know, stakeholders we've created slides for and whatnot
immediate.speaker: Zach Matek

immediate.win: Lift test excited VP of integrated marketing for monthly reporting
immediate.quote: we showed the lift test to our VP of integrated marketing. She was very excited about it, so great timing with, with that, 'cause we're doing some monthly reporting this weekend
immediate.speaker: Zach Matek

immediate.win: Pipeline projections module ready to show CMO
immediate.quote: Our CMO is gonna love this. So, and then what was the differe between pipeline units again and booking?
immediate.speaker: Zach Matek

immediate.win: Channel classification moving in right direction with early start
immediate.quote: definitely, you know, glad we're starting it early and I think it's going the right direction
immediate.speaker: Zach Matek

immediate.win: Channel classification rules now visible in UI for self-service editing
immediate.quote: here's where you'll see the rules that you yourself can edit um at any moment in time right so you kind of see this is a rule criteria and this is what will kind of like um spit out when you based on the logic that you set
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Email click-only attribution rule implemented to reduce inflated email channel
immediate.quote: J put in the like the rule for emails, right? So when the sync completes today, that'll be like just email clicks.
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: Pipeline projection tracking enabled to validate model accuracy over time
immediate.quote: you can see the total projection, your actual generated pipeline, the projection from the fund on the projection versus the you're generated. Yeah, this is exactly what I wrote up for.
immediate.speaker: Zach Matek

immediate.win: Journey timeline now supports stage-to-stage analysis with period filters
immediate.quote: now you can do analyses, right? from one journey stage to another, right? From like discovery to that stage or discovery to like four stages down.
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

long_term.win: Pipeline predictability to enable confident forecasting for board/CMO
long_term.quote: the third piece is the pipeline predictability. And I think if we cover each of those three buckets it'll be a very easy story to tell and show value of this tool
long_term.speaker: Zach Matek

long_term.win: Visual storytelling for late March progress report to leadership
long_term.quote: in late March we need to show kind of a progress report. So we'll be very transparent with you guys that visual storytelling is really gonna help us here
long_term.speaker: Dan Edson

long_term.win: AI attribution model configuration matching DGE views for Markov chain attribution
long_term.quote: what we can do right, is you can create a config here with there's certain inclusions exclusions that you want. And then from that I can go on the backend and create an AI attribution model so that then you can use AI attribution using markoff chains
long_term.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

long_term.win: MQL rescoring based on propensity factors rather than source fields
long_term.quote: we're trying to do MQL rescoring, right, and think about what actually helps or contributes to a more positive, or what should our MQL score be so that we are prioritizing the right leads
long_term.speaker: Zach Matek

before_state.pain_summary: Marketing attribution broken by poor AE/SDR data entry, account matching failures, finance-owned RevOps that dismisses marketing's value, and piecemealed Tableau reports
kpis_unhealthy: partner deal reg shared value proof

after_state.with_revsure: Early implementation with funnel views operational, channel classification in progress, pipeline projections being trained, and stakeholder views being built for CMO presentation
wins_realized: funnel views generating positive stakeholder feedback
lift test exciting integrated marketing VP
lightweight 20-min integrations vs previous heavy-lift tools
Self-service channel classification rules in UI
Email click-only attribution to reduce channel inflation
Pipeline projection tracker validating model accuracy
CEO dashboard with pipeline health and waterfall views
open_issues: pipeline model needs retraining after stage changes
form submission attribution bug being investigated
FCA percentage explanation and credibility with stakeholders
APAC account bias in propensity model
Event attribution timing for list-upload campaigns
Broad marketer adoption beyond single power user
